what_it_does: Defines a single Python dict named BINDING describing a visualization 'lens' that arranges events around a day/week cycle frame; carries an id, label, human-meaning meta block, and three axis-mapping keys (angle_from, radius_from, order_by).
observations: id is 'time-of-day' with label 'Day cycle — activity around the clock'.
meta block holds human-readable name/is/fills/why fields described as registry-true and declared-first.
Comments mark the meta as a TENTATIVE draft to be ratified by Tim/DNA and warn against using machine names.
An inline comment notes the FE cycle-frame switch overrides radius to day/week; this value is only an opening hint.
standouts: Explicit note that the frontend cycle-frame switch overrides radius_from at runtime, so the declared value is just a default hint.
conventions: Uppercase module-level BINDING constant as the export surface.
Separates machine axis keys (angle_from/radius_from/order_by) from a human meta block.
Carries epistemic-status comments (TENTATIVE, ratify) inline with the data.
notes: A declarative lens config: places events on a clock-like day/week cycle, with FE able to override the radius.
questions: What is the full vocabulary of valid values for angle_from / radius_from / order_by?
Who or what is 'DNA' that ratifies the meta?
purpose_vs_actual: Match — the top comment says it binds the radius to the DAY cycle by default, and the dict declares radius_from='time' with a documented day/week override.
apparent_themes: Separation of declared default from runtime override
intention_signals: Tries to register one reusable 'lens' that any event set can be viewed through, framing time cyclically (around a clock) rather than linearly, with the human description authored before machine semantics.
novelty: The cyclic-time framing of events plus an explicit 'this is only an opening hint, FE overrides it' contract on a config value.
contribution: Contributes one cycle-frame binding definition and its human-meaning meta for arranging events around a day/week clock.
summary_for_embedding: A Python BINDING dict defining a 'day cycle' visualization lens that arranges events around a clock-like day/week frame via angle/radius/order axis mappings plus a human-meaning meta block.
intention_for_embedding: To register a reusable cyclic-time lens so events can be viewed by the rhythm of the day or week.
